name: rad-a11y
description: WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility toolkit for Claude Code. Six skills covering semantic HTML, ARIA patterns, keyboard/focus, forms, automated testing setup, and a static-analysis review pass; one autonomous reviewer agent. **2.1 ships four pure-stdlib Python validators** (scan-jsx-patterns, check-tailwind-contrast with real WCAG sRGB math, check-target-size for WCAG 2.5.8, lint-aria wrapping eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y when installed) that the /a11y-review skill and a11y-reviewer agent run before LLM judgment — moving deterministic detection from LLM regex passes to scripts. The /a11y-review skill performs static pattern matching over .tsx/.jsx/.astro/.html/.css source — it is not an audit and does not run axe-core, does not test runtime focus behavior, does not test with screen readers. Findings are tagged [STATIC] (deterministic, from validators), [HEURISTIC] (LLM judgment), or [NEEDS-MANUAL] (browser/AT verification required). Pair with the a11y-testing skill for real axe-core / jest-axe / Playwright integration, and with manual screen reader testing for full coverage. React, Astro, plain HTML, and Tailwind have first-class checks; other stacks fall back to framework-agnostic HTML rules.
